name: Sort and Sweep
summary: Sort by start ascending (ties by larger end first), then sweep once keeping an interval only when its end passes the largest end kept so far.
visualizationIntent: init: show the sorted intervals as cells (each cell an interval like [1, 4]), count 0 and prev_end 0. loop: the i pointer moves to the current interval. update: read the current interval's start and end. check: compare end against prev_end — if end is larger the interval is NOT covered (flash it result-green) and count grows; otherwise it is covered by an earlier interval and is skipped (stays current, no green). return: freeze on the final survivor count.

lineExplanations.3: Sort the intervals so that any interval able to cover another comes first.
lineExplanations.4: Start the survivor counter at zero.
lineExplanations.5: Track the largest right endpoint among intervals we have decided to keep.
lineExplanations.6: Walk through the sorted intervals one by one.
lineExplanations.7: Read the current interval's start endpoint.
lineExplanations.8: Read the current interval's end endpoint.
lineExplanations.9: Keep the interval only if its end reaches beyond every kept interval; otherwise it is covered.
lineExplanations.10: Count this interval because it is not covered.
lineExplanations.11: Remember this interval's end as the new farthest right endpoint.
lineExplanations.12: Return how many intervals were not covered.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the class wrapper the platform expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def removeCoveredIntervals(self, intervals): defines the method; intervals is a list of [start, end] pairs.
syntaxExplanations.3: intervals.sort(key=lambda p: (p[0], -p[1])) sorts by start ascending and, on equal starts, by larger end first.
syntaxExplanations.4: count = 0 creates an integer counter starting at zero.
syntaxExplanations.5: prev_end = 0 stores the largest right endpoint kept so far.
syntaxExplanations.6: for i in range(len(intervals)): loops i over every interval index in sorted order.
syntaxExplanations.7: start = intervals[i][0] reads the left endpoint of interval i.
syntaxExplanations.8: end = intervals[i][1] reads the right endpoint of interval i.
syntaxExplanations.9: if end > prev_end: runs the block only when this interval reaches beyond everything kept.
syntaxExplanations.10: count += 1 adds one to the survivor count.
syntaxExplanations.11: prev_end = end updates the farthest kept right endpoint.
syntaxExplanations.12: return count sends back the number of surviving intervals.
